preflight.description: Verify the Obsidian CLI is available and the target vault can be resolved before any workflow runs.
checks: if the readiness check fails, classify the error as `app_not_running`, `vault_resolution_failed`, or `cli_registration_missing` before retrying
for `app_not_running`, instruct the user to open Obsidian and then retry `obsidian version` once
for `vault_resolution_failed`, require explicit `vault=<name>` or `vault=<id>` and retry the original command
for `cli_registration_missing`, instruct the user to re-enable CLI registration in Obsidian settings before retrying
require explicit `vault=<name>` or `vault=<id>` when the current working directory is not an Obsidian vault

before_write.description: Validate note writes before creating or modifying Markdown files through the Obsidian CLI.
checks: confirm whether the target is a new note, an existing note, or the daily note
prefer exact `path=<path>` targeting when a note name could be ambiguous
validate that generated content is already clean Obsidian Markdown before calling `create`, `append`, or `prepend`
prefer `property:set` for structured metadata when supported, otherwise embed frontmatter in the content body

after_write.description: Perform a lightweight verification pass after a write operation completes.
checks: re-read the target note when possible to confirm the write landed
confirm that important properties or frontmatter fields are present
flag suspicious outcomes such as an empty note, duplicated heading, or missing related-note section

guard_destructive.description: Prevent destructive or high-risk file operations from running silently.
policy: do not execute destructive commands automatically during note capture, research, or backlink workflows
require explicit user confirmation before any destructive file or property mutation
prefer additive edits such as `append`, `prepend`, and `property:set` over destructive rewrites
